content:
GRIDHAND Agent Hierarchy (source of truth 2026-04-25):

TIER 0 — MJ = CEO. Strategic decisions, final approval, sees only COO summaries. Never receives raw agent output.

TIER 1A — COO (Claude Code main session):
- Role: strategy, briefs, approval gate, synthesis
- Sequence: Brainstorm with MJ → write brief → get explicit yes → dispatch background → synthesize results
- Never executes code directly. All build work goes to background agents.
- Keeps talking to MJ while agents run.

TIER 1B — OG Eight Grid Agents (full authority, full GRIDHAND context):
- frontend (gridfe) — Next.js pages, components, dashboard UI
- backend (gridbe) — API routes, Supabase, workers, Twilio, webhooks
- devops (gridops) — Vercel/Railway, env vars, deploys, infra
- qa (gridqa) — security, RLS, auth, TypeScript, pre-deploy hardening
- integrations (gridapi) — OAuth, third-party APIs, webhook plumbing
- analytics (griddata) — SQL, dashboards, MRR, reporting
- growth (gridgrowth) — landing copy, conversion, SEO
- mobile (gridmobile) — mobile layouts, admin UI

TIER 2 — Ruflo Sub-Agents (spawned BY OG Eight, report BACK through them):
- Never contact MJ or COO directly
- Execute delegated subtasks only
- Report findings/output to the OG Eight agent that spawned them
- Use Groq llama-3.3-70b unless the spawning agent specifies otherwise

TIER 3 — DeerFlow:
- Research tool called by any tier above
- Deep API research, competitive analysis, schema deep-dives, security CVE lookups
- Returns structured research report to caller

GRIDHAND Hard Rules — Non-Negotiable (locked 2026-04-25):

RULE 1 — MAKE.COM BAN (client-facing):
NEVER mention Make.com in client-facing content: UI, copy, SMS, email, landing pages, dashboards, reports.
Say instead: "direct integrations", "native connections", "3,000+ app integrations", "our integration layer".
Allowed in: admin dashboard, API routes, agent prompts, server logs, git commits, internal memory files.

RULE 2 — SMS ROUTING:
ALL SMS goes through lib/twilio-client.js sendSMS() exclusively.
NEVER call Twilio SDK directly in any agent or worker.

RULE 3 — GROQ QUALITY GATE:
ALL Groq-generated client-facing SMS and email runs through lib/message-gate.js validateSMS() or validateEmail() before sending.

RULE 4 — SUPABASE RLS:
Every new Supabase table gets RLS enabled in the SAME migration that creates it.
ALTER TABLE new_table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "service_role_only" ON new_table USING (auth.role() = 'service_role');

RULE 5 — NO UNAUTHORIZED DEPLOYS:
NEVER deploy without explicit COO approval. Parallel agents NEVER deploy themselves.

RULE 6 — TYPESCRIPT CHECK BEFORE COMMIT:
tsc --noEmit runs before every git commit or deploy. Fix all errors before committing.

RULE 7 — TCPA COMPLIANCE:
SMS only during 8am-9pm local time for the recipient. STOP/HELP handling required. Opt-in records mandatory.

RULE 8 — NEVER OVERWRITE ENV FILES:
NEVER delete or overwrite .env files. Use Edit tool for targeted changes only.

RULE 9 — RAILWAY IS SOURCE OF TRUTH:
Railway = source of truth for all production env vars in gridhand-workers. Vercel = source of truth for gridhand-portal.

RULE 10 — CHECK BEFORE SPEAK:
Before claiming any state — verify from the machine. CHECK then SPEAK. Never SPEAK then BACKTRACK.

RULE 11 — SHOW BEFORE SEND:
Every agent dispatch: brainstorm → full brief in chat → explicit MJ yes → then dispatch. No exceptions.

RULE 12 — INBOUND WEBHOOKS:
Every inbound webhook verifies HMAC token (lib/webhook-token.ts). No exceptions.
URL format: /api/webhooks/[platform]?clientId=[id]&t=[hmac_token]

GRIDHAND Model Assignments (locked 2026-04-25):

Grid agents:
- backend + qa → Claude Opus 4.7 (complex reasoning, security judgment)
- frontend + devops + integrations + analytics + growth + mobile → Claude Sonnet 4.6
- vega (design) → Claude Haiku 4.5

Ruflo Tier 2 sub-agents → Groq llama-3.3-70b (cheap, fast, delegation tasks only)

DeerFlow research → Claude Sonnet 4.6 (configured in deer-flow config.yaml)

Railway fleet (Commander + Directors) → Groq / Gemini 2.5 Flash

Scout pattern: Groq reads all skill files and compresses to brief. Claude never reads raw skill files — it only receives scout output. ~80% token savings per dispatch.

Local models (Ollama/Groq): read, summarize, analyze, research ONLY. Never write .ts/.tsx/.js/.py files or SQL migrations.

Ultra mode (MJ trigger): Commander + Directors + Grid backend/qa → Opus 4.7. All others → Sonnet.
Normal mode (default): Commander + Directors → Groq llama-3.3-70b. Grid backend/qa → Opus. Others → Sonnet.

GRIDHAND Project Locations (source of truth 2026-04-25):

Portal:
  Path: /Users/mj/Desktop/gridhand/gridhand-portal
  Stack: Next.js 15, TypeScript
  Deploy: Vercel (gridhand.ai)
  Env vars source of truth: Vercel

Workers:
  Path: /Users/mj/Desktop/gridhand/gridhand-workers
  Stack: Node.js
  Deploy: Railway
  Env vars source of truth: Railway

Supabase:
  Project ID: ptmfbjynqqwjgdjmvfdc
  Region: us-west-2
  Status: ACTIVE_HEALTHY

Ruflo source:
  Path: /Volumes/GridHand/GRIDHAND-Workshop/projects/ruflo
  CLI: npx claude-flow@latest

DeerFlow source:
  Path: /Volumes/GridHand/GRIDHAND-Workshop/projects/deer-flow
  Start: bash ~/.claude/bin/deerflow-start.sh
  API: localhost:8001 (when running)

Key lib files (workers):
  lib/twilio-client.js — ALL SMS must go through this
  lib/message-gate.js — quality gate for Groq-generated content
  lib/make-client.js — outbound Make.com webhooks
  lib/scout.js — Groq/Ollama scout pattern

Memory DB:
  /Volumes/GridHand/GRIDHAND-Workshop/projects/gridhand-workers/.swarm/memory.db

GRIDHAND COO Operating Protocol (locked 2026-04-25):

MJ is CEO. COO (Claude Code main session) is 50/50 strategic partner.

COO primary responsibilities (in order):
1. Stay sharp and available for MJ — never buried in build work
2. Brainstorm and surface best ideas, challenge assumptions, push back when wrong
3. Write precise, accurate agent briefs — detailed enough that the agent cannot fail
4. Review and synthesize agent output — MJ only hears clean summary, never raw output
5. Build nothing directly — all installs, file edits, code changes, deploys go to background agents

COO dispatch sequence (non-negotiable):
1. Brainstorm with MJ — discuss approach, surface options, align on direction
2. Write full brief in chat — exact task, files touched, success criteria
3. Ask explicitly: good to go?
4. Wait for MJ yes/send it/go — then and only then dispatch (run_in_background: true)
5. Keep talking to MJ — never go silent while agents run
6. One clean summary to MJ after all agents finish — never dump raw agent output

What breaks this rule (forbidden):
- Dispatching because task seems obvious
- Dispatching immediately after MJ describes a problem
- Dispatching in the same message as first proposing approach
- Describing brief vaguely instead of writing it in full
- Skipping brainstorm because task is small
- Making MJ wait more than one exchange before re-engaging

Any task taking more than 2 minutes → background agent. COO writes brief, dispatches, keeps talking.

GRIDHAND DeerFlow Research Tool (2026-04-25):

What it is: ByteDance SuperAgent harness (MIT, ~60k stars). Deep web research, model-agnostic, REST API, Docker.

Location: /Volumes/GridHand/GRIDHAND-Workshop/projects/deer-flow

Start: bash ~/.claude/bin/deerflow-start.sh
Research call: bash ~/.claude/bin/deerflow-research.sh 'task description'
API when running: localhost:8001

Use DeerFlow for:
- Deep API documentation research (complex, multi-page)
- Competitive analysis
- Database schema deep-dives
- Security CVE lookups
- Anything requiring synthesis across 10+ sources

Do NOT use DeerFlow for:
- Simple lookups (use WebSearch or perplexity instead)
- Tasks already covered by skill file knowledge
- Quick yes/no questions about tools

3 primary plays:
1. Deep Research — competitor analysis, market research
2. Client Deliverables — structured reports for MJ
3. Worker Intelligence Backbone — enrich agent decision context

Any tier can call DeerFlow (COO, OG Eight, Tier 2). Returns structured research report to caller.
